audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as Elihu's speech on righteousness, benefit/harm, oppression cries, songs in the night, unanswered prayer, and Job's alleged empty speech. Marked as Elihu's speech, not final theology.

verses.restored: Do you think this is justice? You say, My righteousness is more than the Divine's.
For you say, What benefit is it to you? What profit do I have more than if I had sinned?
I will answer you with words, and your friends with you.
Look toward the heavens and see; behold the clouds, which are higher than you.
If you sin, what do you do against him? If your transgressions multiply, what do you do to him?
If you are righteous, what do you give him, or what does he receive from your hand?
Your harm affects a person like yourself, and your righteousness a human being.
Because of many oppressions people cry out; they cry for help because of the arm of the great.
But no one says, Where is the Divine my Maker, who gives songs in the night,
who teaches us more than the beasts of the earth and makes us wiser than the birds of the heavens?
There they cry out, but he does not answer, because of the pride of evil people.
Surely emptiness the Divine will not hear, and Shaddai will not regard it.
How much less when you say you do not behold him; the case is before him, and you must wait for him.
But now, because his anger has not punished, and he does not greatly regard transgression,
Job opens his mouth in emptiness; without knowledge he multiplies words.
